description: Nine lines in two different hands; one hand seems to be a chancery hand and the other one is not as elegant. The document starts with a basmala and then becomes relatively difficult to read. The first line reads '..Lawlā l-ḥāl fī l-ḥad li-l-ḥukm'. Contains "innā" as well as "lām" multiple times, possibly "mālik" and "mamluk". Towards the end, the writer also mentions that he doesn't grieve upon something, 'lam yaḥzun ʿalā'.
description_he: תשעה שורות בשתי ידות שונות; אחת נראית כתב קנצלריה; פותח בבסמלה; קשה לקריאה; מזכיר 'לם יחזן עלא'
